summary: Greece's quantum-safe activity is led through the Ministry of Digital Governance and the National Cybersecurity Authority (EAK). The new National Cybersecurity Strategy 2026 to 2030, issued under protocol 2563 of December 2025, explicitly frames the move to post-quantum cryptography as a matter of strategic importance for Greece as an EU member state, and the National Cybersecurity Authority together with the Demokritos research institute has convened the country's first national quantum-era cybersecurity workshop, Q-PrEP 2026, centred on the transition to post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution. Alongside this, the Ministry's observatory registry lists the EU-funded PQ-REACT post-quantum cryptography project and the Ministry has promoted Greece's standing in European quantum communications. The posture is therefore one of an emerging national strategic commitment supported by research and infrastructure participation, but with no published national algorithm list, no stated hybrid deployment position and no dated national migration milestones of its own.
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
govActors:
Ministry of Digital Governance | Greek ministry responsible for digital policy and cyber security
National Cybersecurity Authority (EAK) | Greek national cyber security authority; issued the National Cybersecurity Strategy 2026-2030 and convened the Q-PrEP 2026 quantum-readiness workshop
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
Commission Recommendation (EU) 2024/1101 on a coordinated PQC roadmap | EU | soft-law
NIS Cooperation Group Coordinated Implementation Roadmap (2026/2030/2035) | EU | soft-law
